text: 1 JAMES B. BEAM DISTILLING CO. D YD M /LI C \ BASIL HAYDEN’S BOURBON WHISKEY | KENTUCKY, USA Named after Basil Hayden, a distiller from the 18th century who initially made rye whiskey in Maryland, before moving to the then . : newly-formed state of Kentucky, where he produced a distinctive, 1 high-rye style of Bourbon. This dram is a fitting tribute, said to be S = made with a mashbill of 63% corn, 27% rye, 10% malted barley. Aot 7 A NOSE - A rather soft nose, with subtle sweet aromas of caramel, [ it molasses and dark fruits. A Christmas cake profile comes with time, LS v underpinned by subtle spice and gentle mint. NG L TASTE - Salted caramel emerges on this warming palate, alongside I AT bittersweet cacao nibs and marmalade. Nicely balanced by spicy rye flavours and subtle sweet plums.
